rationale: Social Sciences at level 1 carries everything non-psychology, non-economics, non-business that publishes empirical work. Fourteen subspecialties match how social-science departments are actually organized: sociology, anthropology, political science, education, communication, criminology, geography, linguistics, STS, demography. I split Education Research as its own card (despite some overlap with Cognitive Psychology) because the field has its own conferences and journals and 'education researcher' is a distinct identity. I added Science & Technology Studies (STS) as its own card because the field has matured into a distinct community publishing in Soc Stud Sci, Sci Tech Hum Values, etc.

subspecialties.name: Sociology & Stratification
subspecialties.tagline: Inequality, mobility, family, culture, social networks.
covers: social stratification, inequality and mobility, family sociology, cultural sociology, sociology of religion, social networks methodology, comparative sociology
AJS, ASR, Soc Forces, Soc Sci Res
explicitly_excludes: organizational sociology of firms → Business & Management

subspecialties.name: Political Science
subspecialties.tagline: Comparative politics, IR, voting, public opinion, democratic theory.
covers: comparative politics, international relations, American politics, voting and elections, public opinion, political behavior, political theory empirical
APSR, AJPS, J Pol, IO

subspecialties.name: Education Research & Learning Science
subspecialties.tagline: Pedagogy, learning, curriculum, education policy research.
covers: learning sciences (educational research angle), curriculum research, pedagogy, education policy research, teacher education, higher ed research
AERJ, J Educ Psychol, Educ Res, Rev Educ Res
explicitly_excludes: cognitive psychology of learning → Brain & Cognitive Sciences

subspecialties.name: Anthropology
covers: cultural anthropology, social anthropology, ethnographic research, linguistic anthropology (one home), archaeology-as-anthropology, evolutionary anthropology
Am Anthropol, Curr Anthropol, J Anthropol Res

subspecialties.name: Communication & Media Studies
subspecialties.tagline: Mass communication, media effects, journalism, social media research.
covers: mass communication, media effects research, journalism studies, social media research, political communication, science communication, health communication
J Commun, Communication Research, New Media Soc
explicitly_excludes: NLP-flavored media analysis → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Criminology & Criminal Justice
subspecialties.tagline: Crime causes, policing, courts, corrections, juvenile justice.
covers: crime causation, policing research, courts and corrections, juvenile justice, victimology, white-collar crime, criminological theory
Criminology, J Quant Criminol, J Crim Justice
explicitly_excludes: forensic science → Medicine or Chemistry depending on angle

subspecialties.name: Demography & Population Studies
covers: fertility research, mortality research, migration demography, family demography, demographic methods, aging research demographic
Demography, Pop Stud, J Marr Fam

subspecialties.name: Human Geography & Urban Studies
subspecialties.tagline: Urban planning research, economic geography, GIS social, place.
covers: urban planning research, economic geography, regional science, GIS social, place and space research, transportation geography social
Ann AAG, Urban Stud, Reg Stud

subspecialties.name: Linguistics
covers: theoretical linguistics (syntax, semantics, phonology), sociolinguistics, language documentation, typology, historical linguistics, applied linguistics
Language, Linguistics, J Linguist

subspecialties.name: Science & Technology Studies (STS)
subspecialties.tagline: Sociology of science, history of recent science, science policy.
covers: sociology of science, science policy studies, history of recent science, science and democracy, expertise studies, STS methodology
Soc Stud Sci, STHV, Minerva

subspecialties.name: Public Policy & Administration
subspecialties.tagline: Policy analysis, policy implementation, governance research.
covers: policy analysis, policy implementation research, governance research, regulatory studies (social science angle), comparative public policy
JPAM, J Public Policy, Policy Stud J

subspecialties.name: Social Work & Welfare
subspecialties.tagline: Welfare interventions, child welfare, mental health social work.
covers: social work research, welfare interventions, child welfare, mental health social work, family-focused interventions, gerontological social work

subspecialties.name: Social Science Methodology
subspecialties.tagline: Mixed methods, ethnography, survey methodology, causal social.
covers: mixed-methods research, ethnographic methodology, survey methodology in social science, qualitative research methods, causal inference applied to social
Sociol Methods Res, Field Methods, JEPS
explicitly_excludes: pure stats methods → Statistics & Data Science

subspecialties.name: Computational Social Science
subspecialties.tagline: Big data social, agent-based models, network analysis, social ML.
covers: computational social science, big data in social research, agent-based models social, social network analysis large-scale, ML applied to social science questions
JCSS, Nat Hum Behav comp soc
explicitly_excludes: pure ML methodology → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Law & Legal Studies
subspecialties.tagline: Public, private, international law — doctrine, theory, empirical legal studies.
covers: constitutional and administrative law, private and commercial law, labor and employment law, international and comparative law, empirical legal studies, law and society, jurisprudence
Harv L Rev, Yale LJ, J Legal Stud, J Empirical Legal Stud
subspecialties.source: LoC top-class K / OECD-FOS folds law into social sciences + Per Alex 2026-06-11

uncovered_concerns: Considered Gender Studies as its own card — folds across Sociology, Communication, Political Science depending on lens.
Considered Religion Studies — folds under Sociology, Anthropology, or Humanities.
Considered Bioethics — folds under Public Policy or Humanities (philosophy).
